name: John Cornelius
title: Operations Project & Program Delivery Leader
summary: Operations and project leader with 20+ years across manufacturing and Army aviation, combining operating cadence, project governance, cross-functional execution, and practical analytics. Leads improvement and automation work from problem definition through adoption, with verified line-item savings, clear stakeholder reporting, and disciplined risk control.

Project Delivery: Scope, schedule, risk, and resource coordination
Operations: Production, maintenance, material flow, quality, and safety cadence
Analytics & Automation: Power BI, Python, SQL, MES, ERP, and SCADA data
Leadership: Army aviation standards and risk management

experience.title: AI & Six Sigma Lead / Operations & Quality Leader
duties: Lead or support cross-functional projects across operations, quality, maintenance, IT, controls, finance, and plant leadership.
Manage project cadence for a 40+ project DMAIC and continuous-improvement portfolio tied to $4.2M+ in annualized savings.
Build Power BI and Python analytics workflows for loss analysis, SPC, project tracking, and operational decision support.
Translate technical and analytical work into clear business language for plant managers, functional leaders, and senior stakeholders.
achievements: Reduced blowdown oil waste by 70.8% using BrainCube analytics and airflow optimization, delivering $189K in verified annual savings.
Delivered $37K in verified annual savings through AI-based PID auto-tuning deployed in Python/Jupyter Notebook.
Eliminated 15-minute pre-shift handovers by building an automated Power Apps shift brief system, saving $16.8K per year.
Cut JHA review time by 75% using a ChatGPT API workflow with company-specific vector retrieval.
Managed CAPEX projects and cross-functional teams in process optimization and asset reliability initiatives.

experience.title: Army Aviation Operations & Standardization Leader (Retired CW3)
duties: Led aviation operations and readiness programs involving 30-250+ personnel, equipment accountability, logistics, maintenance coordination, and safety-critical execution.
Managed operating requirements involving $10M+ budgets, aircraft readiness, training resources, scheduling constraints, and risk controls.
Built training, evaluation, standards, and risk-management systems that improved readiness, accountability, and disciplined execution.
achievements: Managed aircrew training and Electronic Flight Bag programs, maintaining current DoD flight information and digital procedures.
Led revision of USAACE Regulation 350-3, consolidating two regulations and streamlining aircraft scheduling.
Mentored 25 pilots to pilot-in-command status while conducting standardization evaluations.

education.degree: M.S. - Unmanned Systems, Human Factors Engineering

affiliations: U.S. Army veteran; community volunteer leadership in Eastern Panhandle, WV
